caveat: Demo key: the signature mechanism is fully real (one flipped byte makes it INVALID), but this private key ships with the repo, so it proves 'signed by whoever holds this demo key', not HSM/KMS-grade secrecy. Key rotation, a published key directory, and RFC-3161 timestamping are Phase 2.

chunks.text:
1. Financial entities shall report major ICT-related incidents to the relevant
competent authority as referred to in Article 46 in accordance with paragraph 4 of
this Article.

Where a financial entity is subject to supervision by more than one national
competent authority referred to in Article 46, Member States shall designate a
single competent authority as the relevant competent authority responsible for
carrying out the functions and duties provided for in this Article.

Credit institutions classified as significant, in accordance with Article 6(4) of
Regulation (EU) No 1024/2013, shall report major ICT-related incidents to the
relevant national competent authority designated in accordance with Article 4 of
Directive 2013/36/EU, which shall immediately transmit that report to the ECB.
chunks.title: Reporting of major ICT-related incidents

chunks.text:
3. Where a major ICT-related incident occurs and has an impact on the financial
interests of clients, financial entities shall, without undue delay as soon as
they become aware of it, inform their clients about the major ICT-related incident
and about the measures that have been taken to mitigate the adverse effects of
such incident.

In the case of a significant cyber threat, financial entities shall, where
applicable, inform their clients that are potentially affected of any appropriate
protection measures which the latter may consider taking.
chunks.title: Notifications and reports to the competent authority

chunks.text:
4. Financial entities shall submit to the relevant competent authority referred to
in Article 46:

(a) an initial notification;

(b) an intermediate report after the initial notification referred to in point
(a), as soon as the status of the original incident has changed significantly or
the handling of the major ICT-related incident has changed based on new
information available, followed, as appropriate, by updated notifications every
time a relevant status update is available, as well as upon a specific request of
the competent authority;

(c) a final report, when the root cause analysis has been completed, regardless of
whether mitigation measures have already been implemented, and when the actual
impact figures are available to replace estimates.
chunks.title: Initial, intermediate, and final reports

chunks.text:
Summary (not verbatim): Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2024/1772 (the RTS
adopted under DORA Article 18(3)) classifies an ICT-related incident as MAJOR when
it meets the materiality thresholds across a set of classification criteria. The
criteria are: clients, financial counterparts and transactions affected; the
reputational impact; the duration and service downtime; the geographical spread,
in particular across the territories of two or more Member States; the data losses
the incident entails, in respect of availability, authenticity, integrity, or
confidentiality of data; the criticality of services affected, including the
financial entity's transactions and operations; and the economic impact, both
direct and indirect, in absolute and relative terms. An incident is major when it
affects critical services and meets either the threshold on clients/counterparts/
transactions together with the data-losses threshold, or two or more of the other
criteria. The precise per-criterion numeric thresholds are set out in the RTS
articles on EUR-Lex (CELEX 32024R1772); this chunk is labelled a summary because
those exact numeric thresholds are not reproduced verbatim here.
chunks.title: Materiality thresholds for classification of major incidents

chunks.citation: Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2024/1772 (reporting time limits, read with DORA Art 19)
chunks.text:
Summary (not verbatim): the reporting time limits for a major ICT-related incident
are set by Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2024/1772 read with the reporting
implementing standards adopted under DORA Article 20. In outline, the initial
notification is due as early as possible and within the time limit set by the
standards once the incident is classified as major; an intermediate report follows
within the time limit set after the initial notification or when the status
changes significantly; and a final report follows within one month of the
intermediate report, once the root-cause analysis is complete and actual impact
figures are available. The Deadline Room models the initial-notification window as
a 72-hour clock from incident occurrence. The exact hour figures are in the
delegated and implementing regulations on EUR-Lex; this chunk is labelled a summary
because those precise figures are not reproduced verbatim here.
chunks.title: Time limits for the initial notification and reports

chunks.text:
1. Each Member State shall ensure that essential and important entities notify,
without undue delay, its CSIRT or, where applicable, its competent authority in
accordance with paragraph 4 of any incident that has a significant impact on the
provision of their services as referred to in paragraph 3 (significant incident).
Where appropriate, entities concerned shall notify, without undue delay, the
recipients of their services of significant incidents that are likely to adversely
affect the provision of those services. Each Member State shall ensure that those
entities report, among other things, any information enabling the CSIRT or, where
applicable, the competent authority to determine any cross-border impact of the
incident. The mere act of notification shall not subject the notifying entity to
increased liability.
chunks.title: Duty to notify significant incidents

chunks.text:
3. An incident shall be considered to be significant if:

(a) it has caused or is capable of causing severe operational disruption of the
services or financial loss for the entity concerned;

(b) it has affected or is capable of affecting other natural or legal persons by
causing considerable material or non-material damage.
chunks.title: When an incident is significant

chunks.text:
4. Member States shall ensure that, for the purpose of notification under
paragraph 1, the entities concerned submit to the CSIRT or, where applicable, the
competent authority:

(a) without undue delay and in any event within 24 hours of becoming aware of the
significant incident, an early warning, which, where applicable, shall indicate
whether the significant incident is suspected of being caused by unlawful or
malicious acts or could have a cross-border impact;

(b) without undue delay and in any event within 72 hours of becoming aware of the
significant incident, an incident notification, which, where applicable, shall
update the information referred to in point (a) and indicate an initial assessment
of the significant incident, including its severity and impact, as well as, where
available, the indicators of compromise;

(c) upon the request of a CSIRT or, where applicable, a competent authority, an
intermediate report on relevant status updates;

(d) a final report not later than one month after the submission of the incident
notification under point (b), including the following:

(i) a detailed description of the incident, including its severity and impact;

(ii) the type of threat or root cause that is likely to have triggered the
incident;

(iii) applied and ongoing mitigation measures;

(iv) where applicable, the cross-border impact of the incident;

(e) in the event of an ongoing incident at the time of the submission of the final
report referred to in point (d), Member States shall ensure that entities
concerned provide a progress report at that time and a final report within one
month of their handling of the incident.
chunks.title: Notification stages and deadlines

chunks.text:
Summary (not verbatim): NIS2 Recital 101 explains that a graduated reporting
timeline (an early warning within 24 hours, a fuller incident notification within
72 hours, and a final report within one month) balances the need for rapid
information to enable the CSIRT or competent authority to react and limit the
incident's spread against the reporting burden on the entity while it is still
responding. The early warning is deliberately light so it can be submitted fast;
the 72-hour notification carries the initial severity and impact assessment and any
indicators of compromise. The exact recital wording is on EUR-Lex (CELEX
32022L2555); this chunk is labelled a summary because the precise recital text is
not reproduced here.
chunks.title: Purpose of the reporting timeline

chunks.text:
(a) Definitions. For purposes of this section: Cybersecurity incident means an
unauthorized occurrence, or a series of related unauthorized occurrences, on or
conducted through a registrant's information systems that jeopardizes the
confidentiality, integrity, or availability of a registrant's information systems
or any information residing therein.
chunks.title: Definition of a cybersecurity incident

chunks.citation: Division of Corporation Finance C&DI Question 104B.01
chunks.text:
Summary (not verbatim): in its Compliance and Disclosure Interpretations on Form
8-K Item 1.05, the SEC's Division of Corporation Finance explains that the
four-business-day filing deadline runs from the registrant's determination that a
cybersecurity incident is MATERIAL, not from the date the incident is discovered.
A registrant must make that materiality determination without unreasonable delay
after discovery. The guidance is interpretive: it confirms the trigger is the
materiality determination, which is why the Deadline Room anchors the SEC clock at
the materiality-determination instant rather than at incident occurrence. The exact
question-and-answer text is published on the SEC's Corporation Finance C&DI page;
this chunk is labelled a summary because that precise wording is not reproduced
verbatim here.
chunks.title: When the four-business-day clock starts

chunks.text:
(a) If the registrant experiences a cybersecurity incident that is determined by
the registrant to be material, describe the material aspects of the nature, scope,
and timing of the incident, and the material impact or reasonably likely material
impact on the registrant, including its financial condition and results of
operations.
chunks.title: Material cybersecurity incidents disclosure

chunks.text:
(b) To the extent that the information called for in paragraph (a) of this item is
not determined or is unavailable at the time of the required filing, the registrant
shall include a statement to this effect in the filing and then must file an
amendment to its Form 8-K filing under this Item 1.05 containing such information
within four business days after the registrant, without unreasonable delay,
determines such information or within four business days after such information
becomes available.
chunks.title: Information not determined or unavailable at the time of filing

chunks.text:
Instruction 1 to Item 1.05. A registrant shall file a report under this Item 1.05
within four business days after the registrant determines that it has experienced a
material cybersecurity incident. The registrant shall provide the information
required by this Item 1.05 in accordance with Rule 12b-20, which requires the
addition of such further material information, if any, as may be necessary to make
the required statements, in light of the circumstances under which they are made,
not misleading.
chunks.title: Timing of the filing

chunks.text:
Instruction 2 to Item 1.05. Notwithstanding General Instruction B.1. to Form 8-K,
if the United States Attorney General determines that disclosure required by
paragraph (a) of this Item 1.05 poses a substantial risk to national security or
public safety, and notifies the Commission of such determination in writing, the
registrant may delay providing the disclosure required by this Item 1.05 for a time
period specified by the Attorney General, up to 30 days following the date when the
disclosure required by this Item 1.05 was otherwise required to be provided.
chunks.title: National security or public safety delay

content_elements.body:
**SEC Breach Notification**  
**Reporting Entity:** Meridian Trust Bank N.V.  
**Incident ID:** inc-8842  
**Notification Date:** [Today's Date]  

**1. Incident Summary**  
On June 16, 2026, at 02:14 UTC, Meridian Trust Bank N.V. detected unauthorized access to its core banking ledger and customer KYC store by the ransomware group LockBit 3.0. The incident remains partially contained.  

**2. Data Compromised**  
Approximately 48,211 records were affected, exposing customer names, addresses, and account numbers. No additional data categories were accessed.  

**3. Response Measures**  
Containment efforts are ongoing. The national CSIRT (NIS2) has been notified per regulatory requirements.  

**4. Regulatory Coordination**  
This filing constitutes the 72-hour notification under applicable SEC rules. Further updates will be provided as the investigation progresses.  

**Contact:** [Designated Point of Contact]  
**Authority Reference:** NIS2 Directive, SEC Reg. S-P

(CLAIMS)
branch=sec
incident_start_utc=2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00
records_affected=48211
attacker=LockBit 3.0
containment=partially_contained
(/CLAIMS)

(CLAIMS)
branch=sec
incident_start_utc=2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00
records_affected=48211
attacker=LockBit 3.0
containment=partially_contained
(/CLAIMS)
content_elements.instruction: State the material aspects of the NATURE of the cybersecurity incident from the fact-record (what occurred, the threat actor, the systems and data categories involved).
content_elements.label: Nature of the incident

edgar-8k-inject_contradiction.json.export_note: EDGAR-shaped Form 8-K Item 1.05 export of the real mandated fields. This is not a filed EDGAR submission: no accession number is assigned and the filer-identifying fields marked as placeholders are supplied by the registrant at submission. The Item 1.05 content elements and the CYD/iXBRL tagging use the real SEC concept names.

    "[5.dora] DORA Drafter draining /next for the mention ...",
    "    DORA Drafter saw the mention (msg m1); calling its assigned model ...",
    "    DORA Drafter drafted 1548 chars, posted back @mention Warden (msg m6)",
    "[6.dora] Warden draining /next for the DORA draft ...",
    "    Warden parsed dora claims, recorded DRAFT_POSTED (msg m6)",
    "    [Warden -> room] @DORA Drafter: recorded DORA filing. Claims: incident_start 2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00, records 48,211, attacker lockbit, containment partially_contained. State: DRAFT_POSTED. (msg m7)",
    "[7] Contradiction diff: BLOCKED. The Warden refused signoff.",
    "        RED: NIS2 says incident_start_utc=2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00; SEC says incident_start_utc=2026-06-16T02:41:00+00:00. Submission blocked.",
    "        RED: SEC says incident_start_utc=2026-06-16T02:41:00+00:00; DORA says incident_start_utc=2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00. Submission blocked.",
    "    [Warden -> room] BLOCKED. @NIS2 Drafter says incident_start_utc=2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00; @SEC Drafter says incident_start_utc=2026-06-16T02:41:00+00:00. No signoff until these agree. (msg m8)",
    "    [SEC Drafter -> NIS2 Drafter] reconcile incident_start_utc: 2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00 vs 2026-06-16T02:41:00+00:00? (msg m9)",
    "    [NIS2 Drafter -> SEC Drafter] confirmed 2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00 canonical per the fact-record (msg m10)",
    "    [SEC Drafter -> Warden] corrected re-filing: incident_start now 2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00 (msg m11)",
    "[7b] Fact corrected on SEC; diff re-run GREEN; signoff unblocked.",
    "    [Warden -> room] Resolved. @SEC Drafter re-filed incident_start 2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00. Diff re-run GREEN across 3 filings. Opening signoff. (msg m12)",
    "    [release] inc-8842:nis2: general_counsel (gc) signed; release withheld: awaiting head_of_ir (have general_counsel)",
    "    [release] inc-8842:nis2: head_of_ir (lena) signed; both keys present, release admitted",
    "    [release] inc-8842:sec: general_counsel (gc) signed; release withheld: awaiting head_of_ir (have general_counsel)",
    "    [release] inc-8842:sec: head_of_ir (lena) signed; both keys present, release admitted",
    "    [release] inc-8842:dora: general_counsel (gc) signed; release withheld: awaiting head_of_ir (have general_counsel)",
    "    [release] inc-8842:dora: head_of_ir (lena) signed; both keys present, release admitted",
    "    [Warden -> room] GC (general_counsel) signed NIS2, SEC, DORA. First of two keys on each. Awaiting LENA. (msg m13)",
    "    [Warden -> room] LENA (head_of_ir) signed NIS2, SEC, DORA. Both keys present on all 3. RELEASED, clocks stopped. (msg m14)",
    "[8] Warden opened signoff; two-key release (GC + Lena); clocks stopped",
    "[9] Replay byte-identical: True (sha f1f2223aa57b...)",
    "    Signed by Deadline Warden (ed25519, key fp 46e30f5bff8c221d) over sha256 + chain_head + attestation_sha + fact_record_hash; the signature is detached, the run-log sha is unchanged.",
    "    Operability: 3 filing(s), nearest deadline 2026-06-17T02:14:00+00:00, min statutory margin 69.23h, 0 breach(es)."
  ],

      "text": "**To:** national CSIRT (NIS2)\n**From:** Meridian Trust Bank N.V.\n**Subject:** NIS2 Mandatory Incident Notification\n**Incident Reference:** inc-8842\n\n**1. Notification Timelines**\nThis notification is submitted within 72 hours of the bank becoming aware of a significant incident, in accordance with Article 23 of the NIS2 Directive. The incident commenced on 16 June 2026 at 02:14 UTC.\n\n**2. Nature and Significance of the Incident**\nThe incident involves a cyber attack attributed to the LockBit 3.0 ransomware group. The incident is currently assessed as partially contained. The attack has affected the availability of the core banking ledger and the customer KYC store systems.\n\n**3. Impact Assessment**\nPreliminary assessment indicates that 48,211 records have been affected. The compromised data categories include customer name, address, and account number.\n\n**4. Measures Taken and Next Steps**\nContainment activities are ongoing. The bank's incident response team is actively engaged in forensic analysis, system remediation, and implementing additional security controls to prevent further unauthorized access. A full impact assessment is in progress.\n\n(CLAIMS)\nbranch=nis2\nincident_start_utc=2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00\nrecords_affected=48211\nattacker=LockBit 3.0\ncontainment=partially_contained\n(/CLAIMS)\n\n(CLAIMS)\nbranch=nis2\nincident_start_utc=2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00\nrecords_affected=48211\nattacker=LockBit 3.0\ncontainment=partially_contained\n(/CLAIMS)\n\n[CLAIMS]\nbranch=nis2\nincident_start_utc=2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00\nrecords_affected=48211\nattacker=LockBit 3.0\ncontainment=partially_contained\n[/CLAIMS]"
    },
    {
      "regime": "SEC",

      "model": "deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3-0324",
      "provider": "featherless",
      "rationale": "DeepSeek-V3-0324 holds SEC in dev as a separate open checkpoint from the NIS2 drafter, so two branches draft from genuinely different model state even before the prod split.",
      "text": "**SEC Breach Notification**  \n**Reporting Entity:** Meridian Trust Bank N.V.  \n**Incident ID:** inc-8842  \n**Notification Date:** [Today's Date]  \n\n**1. Incident Summary**  \nOn June 16, 2026, at 02:14 UTC, Meridian Trust Bank N.V. detected unauthorized access to its core banking ledger and customer KYC store by the ransomware group LockBit 3.0. The incident remains partially contained.  \n\n**2. Data Compromised**  \nApproximately 48,211 records were affected, exposing customer names, addresses, and account numbers. No additional data categories were accessed.  \n\n**3. Response Measures**  \nContainment efforts are ongoing. The national CSIRT (NIS2) has been notified per regulatory requirements.  \n\n**4. Regulatory Coordination**  \nThis filing constitutes the 72-hour notification under applicable SEC rules. Further updates will be provided as the investigation progresses.  \n\n**Contact:** [Designated Point of Contact]  \n**Authority Reference:** NIS2 Directive, SEC Reg. S-P\n\n(CLAIMS)\nbranch=sec\nincident_start_utc=2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00\nrecords_affected=48211\nattacker=LockBit 3.0\ncontainment=partially_contained\n(/CLAIMS)\n\n(CLAIMS)\nbranch=sec\nincident_start_utc=2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00\nrecords_affected=48211\nattacker=LockBit 3.0\ncontainment=partially_contained\n(/CLAIMS)\n\n[CLAIMS]\nbranch=sec\nincident_start_utc=2026-06-16T02:41:00+00:00\nrecords_affected=48211\nattacker=LockBit 3.0\ncontainment=partially_contained\n[/CLAIMS]"

      "text": "Incident ID: inc-8842\n\nDate of Notification: [Insert Date]\n\nRegulated Entity: Meridian Trust Bank N.V.\n\nCompetent Authority: National CSIRT (NIS2)\n\nIncident Start Time: June 16, 2026, at 02:14 UTC\n\nDescription of Incident:\nOn June 16, 2026, at 02:14 UTC, Meridian Trust Bank N.V. experienced a cyber-attack by the ransomware group LockBit 3.0. The attack partially compromised our core banking ledger and customer KYC store. A total of 48,211 records were affected, including personal data such as names, addresses, and account numbers.\n\nContainment Status:\nThe incident is currently partially contained. Our incident response team is actively working to fully contain and mitigate the impact of the attack.\n\nNext Steps:\nWe are conducting a thorough investigation to determine the full extent of the breach and to identify any additional vulnerabilities. We will continue to work with law enforcement and cybersecurity experts to ensure a comprehensive response. Affected customers will be notified in accordance with applicable regulations.\n\nContact Information:\nFor further information or inquiries, please contact our incident response team at [Insert Contact Information].\n\nSincerely,\n\n[Your Name]\n[Your Position]\nMeridian Trust Bank N.V.\n\n(CLAIMS)\nbranch=dora\nincident_start_utc=2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00\nrecords_affected=48211\nattacker=LockBit 3.0\ncontainment=partially_contained\n(/CLAIMS)\n\n(CLAIMS)\nbranch=dora\nincident_start_utc=2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00\nrecords_affected=48211\nattacker=LockBit 3.0\ncontainment=partially_contained\n(/CLAIMS)\n\n[CLAIMS]\nbranch=dora\nincident_start_utc=2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00\nrecords_affected=48211\nattacker=LockBit 3.0\ncontainment=partially_contained\n[/CLAIMS]"
